failure_modes.text: Passive exposure can create brittle understanding because people can repeat the wording without generating the reasoning for themselves.
failure_modes.source_quote: Most useful when retention, understanding, or alignment depends on people producing the explanation, hypothesis, or plan themselves instead of merely hearing it.
failure_modes.mitigation: Require learners to generate explanations, not just recognize or repeat them; passive exposure creates brittle understanding.

failure_modes.text: Complex ideas can remain shallow when teams never have to convert them into their own explanation, concept map, or plan.
failure_modes.source_quote: The Generation Effect is most powerful when dealing with complexity and abstraction.
failure_modes.mitigation: Have teams convert complex ideas into their own concept maps, summaries, or plans before accepting that understanding has occurred.

failure_modes.text: Self-generation can turn into bluffing if it replaces expert correction, evidence checks, or external calibration.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when "generate your own answer" becomes an excuse to skip expert correction, evidence checks, or external calibration.
failure_modes.mitigation: Pair self-generation with expert correction and evidence checks; generating answers without calibration can entrench errors.

premortem_questions.text: Can the team generate the explanation or plan in its own words, or is it only borrowing polished language it has heard before?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Most useful when retention, understanding, or alignment depends on people producing the explanation, hypothesis, or plan themselves instead of merely hearing it.

premortem_questions.text: What evidence check or outside correction are we skipping because self-generated confidence feels satisfying enough?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Danger when "generate your own answer" becomes an excuse to skip expert correction, evidence checks, or external calibration.

premortem_questions.text: If this explanation had to survive an analogy test, where would its weak spots or hidden simplifications show up first?

heuristics.text: Make people generate the explanation, hypothesis, or plan themselves instead of only consuming the finished version.
heuristics.source_quote: Most useful when retention, understanding, or alignment depends on people producing the explanation, hypothesis, or plan themselves instead of merely hearing it.

heuristics.text: Use concept maps, chunking, or structured articulation to turn abstract material into generated understanding.
heuristics.source_quote: Generating a concept map helps organize material and make clear the connections between different ideas.

heuristics.text: Separate discovery from delivery so the hard generation work sharpens understanding without overloading the final communication.

curation_notes.summary: Focused on active articulation, concept generation, and external calibration rather than on broad brainstorming language.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor creativity-workshop overlays because the raw source is already strongest on memory, understanding, and self-generated explanation.
open_questions: The source is also useful for communication design, but the intervention chunks stay anchored to generation as a comprehension mechanism.

allies: The raw allies section is strongest on cognitive-load-theory, feynman-technique, and structured explanation.
antagonists: The source strongly resists fast-thinking fluency and unsupported self-generated certainty.
structured_tensions: The model has clean tension around skipping calibration or confusing self-produced wording with validated understanding.
